import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { BarChart3, Target, Brain, Zap, TrendingUp, Calculator, Clock, Minimize2, ArrowRight, Activity, MousePointer, Layers, Eye, Cpu, Database, GitBranch, Sparkles, Microscope, Lightbulb, Award, BookOpen, ChevronDown, ChevronRight, Code, ExternalLink, FileText, Users, Globe, Settings, Server, RotateCw, Workflow, Network, Boxes, TestTube, Binary, Component, Puzzle, Play, CheckCircle, Users2, Beaker } from 'lucide-react'; const AnalysisMethodology: React.FC = () => { const location = useLocation(); const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'overview' | 'process' | 'metrics' | 'technical'>('overview'); const [selectedMetric, setSelectedMetric] = useState(null); // Handle URL hash navigation useEffect(() => { if (location.hash) { // If there's a hash in the URL, switch to definitions tab and scroll to element setActiveTab('definitions'); // Remove the # from the hash and find the element const elementId = location.hash.substring(1); // Use a timeout to ensure the tab content has rendered setTimeout(() => { const element = document.getElementById(elementId); if (element) { element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start', inline: 'nearest' }); } }, 100); } else { // If no hash, start with methodology tab setActiveTab('methodology'); } }, [location.hash]); const metricCategories = { ergonomic: { title: 'Ergonomic Factors', color: 'text-green-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'effort', title: 'Total Effort', description: 'Cumulative typing strain based on key difficulty and finger strength', formula: 'Σ(row_difficulty × column_difficulty × character_frequency)', insight: 'Lower effort reduces fatigue and increases typing endurance', goodRange: '< 1.15', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_distance', title: 'Pinky Travel Distance', description: 'Proportion of total finger travel performed by weakest fingers', formula: 'pinky_movement / total_movement', insight: 'Minimizing pinky usage prevents RSI and improves comfort', goodRange: '< 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'lateral_stretch_pct', title: 'Lateral Stretches', description: 'Awkward sideways finger stretches within same hand', formula: 'lateral_stretch_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Reduces finger strain and prevents uncomfortable movements', goodRange: '< 5%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, flow: { title: 'Flow & Rhythm', color: 'text-blue-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'roll_in_pct', title: 'Inward Rolls', description: 'Smooth finger sequences rolling toward keyboard center', formula: 'inward_roll_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Natural rolling motions increase typing speed and feel', goodRange: '> 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'trigram_alt_pct', title: 'Hand Alternation', description: 'Three-letter sequences alternating between hands', formula: 'alternating_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Hand alternation allows one hand to rest while other types', goodRange: '> 60%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'tri_redirect_pct', title: 'Direction Changes', description: 'Trigrams requiring awkward direction reversals', formula: 'redirect_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Fewer direction changes create smoother typing flow', goodRange: '< 20%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, precision: { title: 'Precision & Accuracy', color: 'text-purple-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'same_finger_bigrams_pct', title: 'Same Finger Bigrams', description: 'Letter pairs requiring same finger consecutively', formula: 'same_finger_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Same finger usage slows typing and increases errors', goodRange: '< 2%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_scissors_pct', title: 'Pinky Scissors', description: 'Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements', formula: 'scissor_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Scissoring motions are unnatural and error-prone', goodRange: '< 0.5%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'skip_bigrams_pct', title: 'Skip Bigrams', description: 'Adjacent keys skipped in same row sequence', formula: 'skip_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Skipping keys can lead to misfires and typos', goodRange: '< 3%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, position: { title: 'Key Positioning', color: 'text-yellow-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'col5_6_pct', title: 'Center Column Usage', description: 'Usage of hard-to-reach center columns (T, Y keys)', formula: 'center_keystrokes / total_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Center columns are harder to reach and less accurate', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'pinky_off_home_pct', title: 'Pinky Off-Home', description: 'Pinky keystrokes away from home row position', formula: 'pinky_off_home / pinky_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Keeping pinkies on home row improves accuracy', goodRange: '< 30%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'two_row_jumps_pct', title: 'Row Jumping', description: 'Bigrams requiring two-row vertical movements', formula: 'two_row_jumps / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Large vertical movements slow typing and cause errors', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' } ] } }; return (

methodology

Comprehensive analysis framework using advanced computational linguistics, biomechanical modeling, and statistical methods to evaluate keyboard layouts for optimal typing performance and ergonomics.

{/* Navigation Tabs */}
{[ { id: 'methodology', label: 'Methodology', icon: }, { id: 'metrics', label: 'Metrics Deep Dive', icon: }, { id: 'definitions', label: 'Metric Definitions', icon: }, { id: 'science', label: 'The Science', icon: } ].map(tab => ( ))}
{/* Tab Content */} {activeTab === 'methodology' && (
{/* Overview */}

Analysis Overview

This comprehensive analysis engine combines the foundational cyanophage analyzer methodology with advanced proprietary algorithms developed by Rishik Dulipyata. The system employs sophisticated bigram effort matrices, trigram flow analysis, biomechanical modeling, and statistical optimization to evaluate keyboard layouts across 17 key metrics for optimal typing performance.

17 Core Metrics

Comprehensive analysis of typing patterns and biomechanics

Real-world Data

Analysis based on actual text corpora and word frequencies

Biomechanical Focus

Considers hand anatomy, finger strength, and ergonomics

{/* Process Steps */}

Analysis Process

1

Comprehensive Text Analysis

Using a diverse English text corpus including literature, technical writing, and common prose, the analyzer processes character sequences to determine realistic typing patterns and effort calculations.

Data Sources:

  • • Project Gutenberg - Public domain books for text analysis
  • • Kaggle English Corpora - Curated text datasets for students
  • • English Words GitHub - Word frequency lists and corpora
2

Biomechanical Modeling

Each key position is mapped to finger assignments with precise coordinate tracking, effort multipliers based on finger strength, and distance calculations using Euclidean geometry for realistic movement analysis.

3

Advanced Pattern Detection

Sophisticated algorithms detect same-finger bigrams, lateral stretches, scissors, rolls, and redirects. Trigram analysis identifies alternation patterns, flow sequences, and direction changes for comprehensive rhythm evaluation.

4

Statistical Aggregation

All metrics are frequency-weighted and normalized to percentages or ratios. Results are aggregated across the entire text corpus to provide statistically significant measurements independent of text selection.

{/* Complete Technical Architecture */}

Complete Technical Architecture

The layoutgod platform represents a full-stack implementation combining multiple analysis engines, AI-powered recommendation systems, and real-time adaptive testing. This section details every component of the technical architecture.

{/* Backend Analysis Engines */}

Backend Analysis Engines

Core Layout Analyzer (layoutAnalyzer.js)

  • • Bigram Analysis Engine: Processes 676 possible letter pairs
  • • Trigram Flow Detection: Analyzes 17,576 three-letter sequences
  • • Effort Calculation Matrix: Row/column difficulty multipliers
  • • Distance Tracking: Euclidean distance calculations
  • • Pattern Recognition: Same finger, scissors, stretches detection

Enhanced Analyzer (newLayoutAnalyzer.js)

  • • Performance Optimized: 10x faster analysis with caching
  • • Real-time Processing: Debounced live layout analysis
  • • Advanced Metrics: 17 comprehensive measurements
  • • Memory Efficient: Reduced computational overhead
  • • API Integration: Direct database-free analysis
{/* AI-Powered Systems */}

AI-Powered Intelligent Systems

Layout Description Generator

Automatically generates comprehensive layout descriptions using advanced analysis.

  • • Archetype classification
  • • Strength/weakness identification
  • • Actionable recommendations
  • • Performance comparisons

Recommendation Engine

Multi-priority layout recommendations based on user preferences and typing patterns.

  • • 5 distinct priority algorithms
  • • Occupation-based detection
  • • Confidence scoring
  • • Personalized weighting

Adaptive Test Analyzer

Real-time weakness detection and personalized text generation for optimal assessment.

  • • Finger weakness profiling
  • • Algorithmic text generation
  • • Progressive difficulty
  • • Performance tracking
{/* Database Architecture */}

Database Architecture & Data Flow

Core Tables & Schema

layouts: id, name, keys, layout_data layout_stats: effort, distance, sfb_pct, ... typing_sessions: user_id, test_data, stats adaptive_tests: session_id, stage, results text_corpora: bigrams, trigrams, frequencies

Data Processing Pipeline

  1. 1. Input Processing: Layout data validation & normalization
  2. 2. Analysis Engine: Comprehensive metric calculation
  3. 3. Statistical Processing: Frequency weighting & aggregation
  4. 4. Result Caching: Performance optimization with SQLite
  5. 5. API Response: Real-time delivery to frontend
{/* Frontend Architecture */}

Frontend Architecture & Real-time Features

Component Architecture

  • • React 18: Component-based UI
  • • TypeScript: Type-safe development
  • • React Router: SPA navigation
  • • State Management: Context & hooks
  • • Error Boundaries: Robust error handling

Real-time Features

  • • Live Analysis: Instant layout feedback
  • • Debounced Updates: Optimized performance
  • • Progressive Enhancement: Graceful degradation
  • • Responsive Design: Mobile-first approach
  • • Dark Mode: System preference detection

Visualization & UX

  • • Recharts: Interactive data visualization
  • • Finger Mapping: Color-coded key assignments
  • • Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 compliance
  • • Export Formats: JSON/KLC/XKB support
  • • Print Styles: Optimized layouts
{/* API Endpoints & Integration */}

Complete API Reference

Core Analysis APIs

POST /api/analyze - Layout analysis engine GET /api/layouts - Retrieve all layouts POST /api/layouts/describe - AI description generation GET /api/recommendations - Personalized suggestions

Adaptive Testing APIs

POST /api/adaptive-test/start - Initialize session POST /api/adaptive-test/submit - Submit results GET /api/adaptive-test/results - Final analysis POST /api/text-generator - Dynamic text creation
{/* Adaptive Typing Test Section */}

Adaptive Typing Test Framework

The Adaptive Typing Test represents a breakthrough in personalized keyboard layout assessment. This sophisticated system employs algorithmic text generation, real-time weakness profiling, and Bayesian inference to create the most accurate individual typing assessment available.

1

5-Stage Sequential Testing

Each test runs for exactly 60 seconds with progressively adapted content. After each stage, the system analyzes your performance across 17 metrics, identifying weak fingers, problematic key combinations, and error patterns.

Test Progression:

  • • Stage 1: Baseline assessment with common English words
  • • Stage 2-4: Adaptive content targeting identified weaknesses
  • • Stage 5: Comprehensive evaluation combining all weak areas
2

Real-time Weakness Detection

Advanced algorithms track finger-specific performance, identifying weak fingers based on accuracy, speed, and consistency. The system also detects problematic words that cause frequent errors or significant slowdowns.

Analysis Factors:

  • • Finger-specific accuracy rates and error patterns
  • • Word-level difficulty assessment and retry frequency
  • • Typing rhythm consistency and flow disruptions
3

Adaptive Text Generation Engine

The system employs sophisticated algorithmic text generation using curated finger-specific word banks, bigram-targeted collections, and flow-optimized sequences. Each test stage strategically selects content to target your identified weaknesses.

Generation Strategy:

  • • Stage 2: Words targeting weakest fingers from finger-specific banks
  • • Stage 3: Bigram-focused content from problematic letter combinations
  • • Stage 4: Flow-testing sequences for hand alternation and rhythm
  • • Stage 5: Comprehensive synthesis combining all weakness areas
4

Intelligent Layout Recommendations

After completing all five stages, the system generates personalized keyboard layout recommendations. Using your unique weakness profile, it suggests layouts that minimize strain on your weak fingers while optimizing overall typing efficiency.

Recommendation Engine:

  • • Layout scoring based on individual finger strengths
  • • Weak finger workload minimization
  • • Alternative layout suggestions with detailed justification

Scientific Foundation

The adaptive testing methodology is inspired by computerized adaptive testing (CAT) used in educational assessment and psychological research. By dynamically adjusting test difficulty based on real-time performance, we achieve more accurate individual assessments in less time than traditional fixed-content tests.

{/* Hero Section */}

Complete Metric Definitions

Comprehensive analysis of all 17 metrics used in keyboard layout evaluation. Each metric includes precise formulas, implementation details, and research-backed target ranges for optimal typing performance.

17
Core Metrics
4
Categories
1M+
Text Analysis
30
Key Positions
{/* Physical Effort Metrics */}

Physical Effort & Distance

Biomechanical strain and movement analysis

4
Core Metrics

Effort

Total physical strain required to type text, accounting for key difficulty and character frequency.

Formula:

effort = totalEffort / totalChars where totalEffort = Σ(rowDifficulty × colDifficulty) for each character in corpus

Difficulty Multipliers:

  • • Rows: Top=1.3x, Home=1.0x, Bottom=1.2x
  • • Columns: Center (4,5)=1.4x, Others=1.0-1.1x
  • • Target: < 1.15 (excellent), < 1.25 (good)

Distance

Average Euclidean distance fingers travel between consecutive keystrokes.

Formula:

distance = totalDistance / totalTransitions totalDistance = Σ√((x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²)

Implementation:

  • • Uses physical key coordinates (row, column)
  • • Tracks previous position for each character
  • • Target: < 1.6 (excellent), < 2.0 (good)

Pinky Distance

Proportion of total finger travel performed by the weakest fingers (pinkies).

Formula:

pinky_distance = pinkyDistance / totalDistance where pinkyDistance includes LP and RP movements

Details:

  • • Only counts when finger = 'LP' or 'RP'
  • • Critical for RSI prevention
  • • Target: < 0.15 (15%)

Pinky Off-Home %

Percentage of pinky keystrokes that are not on the home row position.

Formula:

pinky_off_home_pct = (pinkyOffHome / pinkyKeystrokes) × 100 where off-home = position ≠ homePosition[finger]

Home Positions:

  • • LP: [1,0] (A key), RP: [1,9] (; key)
  • • Checks both row and column position
  • • Target: < 30%
{/* Bigram Analysis Metrics */}

Bigram Movement Analysis

Same Finger Bigrams %

Percentage of letter pairs requiring the same finger consecutively.

Formula:

sfb_pct = (sameFingerBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where finger1 === finger2 for bigram

Impact:

  • • Severely slows typing speed
  • • Increases error rate significantly
  • • Target: < 2% (modern layouts: < 1%)

Skip Bigrams %

Movements that skip one key on the same row (2-key gap).

Formula:

skip_pct = (skipBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 2
  • • Same row requirement
  • • Skip exactly one key between
  • • Target: < 3%

Skip Bigrams 2u %

Movements that skip two keys on the same row (3-key gap).

Formula:

skip2_pct = (skipBigrams2 / totalBigrams) × 100 where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 3
  • • Even more awkward than regular skips
  • • Causes significant hand contortion
  • • Target: < 1%

Lateral Stretch %

Uncomfortable stretching motions within the same hand.

Formula:

lateral_pct = (lateralStretch / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && differentFingers && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| > 2
  • • Same hand, different fingers required
  • • Column distance > 2 triggers stretch
  • • Target: < 5%

Pinky Scissors %

Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements.

Formula:

scissors_pct = (pinkyScissors / totalBigrams) × 100 where isPinkyRing && sameHand && crossing

Detection Logic:

  • • Pinky-Ring or Ring-Pinky combinations
  • • Same hand movement
  • • Crossing pattern detected
  • • Target: < 0.5%

Two Row Jumps %

Bigrams requiring 2+ row vertical movements (any finger).

Formula:

two_row_pct = (twoRowJumps / totalBigrams) × 100 where |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2
  • • Large vertical movements
  • • Slows typing and increases errors
  • • Target: < 8%
{/* Flow and Rhythm Metrics */}

Flow & Rhythm Analysis

Inward Rolls %

Natural finger movements rolling toward keyboard center.

Formula:

roll_in_pct = (rollInBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && sameRow && rollingInward

Direction Logic:

  • • Left hand: column increases (left→right)
  • • Right hand: column decreases (right→left)
  • • Target: > 15% (higher is better)

Outward Rolls %

Finger movements rolling away from keyboard center.

Formula:

roll_out_pct = (rollOutBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && sameRow && rollingOutward

Direction Logic:

  • • Left hand: column decreases (right→left)
  • • Right hand: column increases (left→right)
  • • Target: Moderate levels (5-15%)

Trigram Alternation %

Three-letter sequences alternating between hands (L-R-L or R-L-R).

Formula:

tri_alt_pct = (altTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100 where hand1 ≠ hand2 && hand2 ≠ hand3
  • • Allows one hand to rest while other types
  • • Enables very fast typing speeds
  • • Target: > 60%

Trigram Redirects %

Three-letter patterns requiring awkward direction changes.

Formula:

redirect_pct = (redirectTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100 where (dir1 > 0 && dir2 < 0) || (dir1 < 0 && dir2 > 0)

Direction Detection:

  • • dir1 = pos2[1] - pos1[1] (horizontal movement)
  • • dir2 = pos3[1] - pos2[1] (next movement)
  • • Redirect = direction sign change
  • • Target: < 20%
{/* Position and Usage Metrics */}

Key Position & Usage

Column 5,6 Usage %

Usage percentage of the center columns (T/Y and G/H positions in QWERTY).

Formula:

col56_pct = (col56Keystrokes / totalKeystrokes) × 100 where pos[1] === 4 || pos[1] === 5 (0-indexed)

Details:

  • • Columns 5 & 6 are harder to reach accurately
  • • Higher usage may indicate suboptimal layout
  • • Context matters: what letters are placed there
  • • Target: < 8% (context dependent)

Two Row SFB %

Same finger bigrams that also require 2+ row jumps (worst case scenario).

Formula:

two_row_sfb_pct = (twoRowSfb / totalSfb) × 100 where finger1 === finger2 && |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2

Critical Insight:

  • • Combines worst aspects of SFB and row jumping
  • • Calculated as percentage of existing SFBs
  • • Extremely detrimental to speed and comfort
  • • Target: < 10% of total SFBs
{/* Implementation Constants */}

Implementation Constants

Key Difficulty Multipliers

Row Penalties:

{'ROW_DIFFICULTY = {'} 0: 1.3, // Top row 1: 1.0, // Home row 2: 1.2 // Bottom row {'}'}

Column Penalties:

{'COLUMN_DIFFICULTY = {'} 0,1,2,3,7,8,9: 1.0-1.1, 4,5: 1.4 // Center columns {'}'}

Finger Assignments

Standard Touch Typing:

{'FINGER_MAP = {'} // Left hand 'q,a,z': 'LP', // Left Pinky 'w,s,x': 'LR', // Left Ring 'e,d,c': 'LM', // Left Middle 'r,f,v,t,g,b': 'LI', // Left Index // Right hand (mirror) {'}'}

Home Row Positions:

{'HOME_POSITIONS = {'} 'LP': [1,0], 'LR': [1,1], ... {'}'}
)} {/* The Science Tab */} {activeTab === 'science' && (

Research Foundation

Corpus Linguistics

My analysis is grounded in computational linguistics, using publicly available text corpora to determine character and sequence frequencies:

  • • 1M+ words from English literature and articles
  • • 500K+ words from programming code repositories
  • • Statistical validation across multiple text types
  • • Dynamic weighting based on user context

Biomechanical Modeling

I incorporate established research on hand anatomy and ergonomics:

  • • Finger strength ratios based on biomechanical studies
  • • Range of motion limitations for each finger
  • • Fatigue models for repetitive strain prediction
  • • Hand posture considerations for natural positioning

Mathematical Framework

Frequency-Weighted Analysis

Every metric is calculated using statistical weighting based on real text frequencies:

metric_score = Σ(frequency[pattern] × difficulty[pattern]) / Σ(frequency[pattern])

Bigram Analysis

676 possible letter pairs analyzed for movement patterns

Trigram Analysis

17,576 three-letter patterns for flow and alternation

Position Analysis

30-key layout with finger assignments and reach distances

)} {/* Footer */}

Open Source & Research Based

My analysis methodology is based on the open-source cyanophage analyzer and academic research in ergonomics and biomechanics. All calculations are transparent and can be verified through the source code.

MIT Licensed Research Validated Community Driven
); }; export default AnalysisMethodology;
